mnemonics: SPELL IT OUT: Spellcheck, POS Tagging, Emotion (Sentiment), Lemmatization, Lexicon, Indexing, Tokenization, Out-of-Vocabulary handling, Understanding context, Disambiguation.
keyPoints: Tokenization: The process of breaking text into words, phrases, or other meaningful elements (tokens).
Part-of-Speech Tagging: Assigning grammatical categories (like noun, verb, adjective) to each word.
Named Entity Recognition: Identifying and classifying named entities (e.g., persons, organizations, locations) in text.
commonMistakes: Assuming tokenization is trivial; it fails on contractions (e.g., 'don't' vs 'do' and 'n't') and compound words.
Confusing lemmatization (which uses vocabulary and morphology to return a base word) with stemming (which simply chops off word endings).
Treating all unknown words (Out-of-Vocabulary words) the same way without a strategy like subword tokenization or using a special <UNK> token.
